title: UAE looking to buy India's BrahMos, Akashteer air defence system: Report
content: India is in advanced discussions with the UAE for a significant defence deal, potentially including the sale of BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer air defence systems. This move signifies deepening strategic ties and India's growing prowess as a defence exporter. The UAE's interest stems from a desire to bolster its military capabilities amidst regional security concerns, marking a potential major win for India's indigenous defence industry.

title: ‘Zyada soch mat’: Sooryavanshi reveals coach’s message before record-breaking knock
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi bounced back from a lean patch to smash a record-breaking 94 off 29 balls in the Tri-Series final against Sri Lanka A. A pep talk from coach Hrishikesh Kanitkar encouraged him to play his natural game, leading to the fastest List A half-century ever. His explosive innings secured the title for India A, showcasing his immense talent despite earlier scrutiny.

title: Lucknow gaming zone fire: 4 charred to death, several injured as rescue op continues
content: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj area as a devastating fire engulfed a coaching-cum-gaming institute, leaving four feared dead and several injured. Panic ensued as the blaze spread rapidly, trapping individuals inside. Authorities launched a massive rescue operation, with the chief minister directing immediate on-site supervision and ensuring aid for victims. An investigation into the cause and safety lapses is underway.

title: Viral 'Europe 2031' scenario warns EU will be 'torn apart' by US and China
content: A viral thought experiment called Europe 2031 warns the EU's 27 nations risk being "torn apart" by the US and China within years, as America hoards AI compute and China dominates robotics. The Brussels scenario, now read by MEPs and policymakers, urges Europe to build datacentres fast. Critics note several cited AI mega-deals have already collapsed, but its authors insist the broad warning still holds.

title: 'Speed up your calculation': Gukesh advised ahead of World Championship match
content: Ahead of his world title defence against Javokhir Sindarov, D Gukesh has been advised by Grandmaster Krishnan Sasikiran to improve his speed, technique and handling of time pressure. Sasikiran warned that Sindarov is highly prepared and fast-paced, but said Gukesh's previous world championship experience could prove crucial in what promises to be a tough contest.

title: Inside Chhattisgarh's secret ganja corridor that no one is talking about
content: Chhattisgarh is emerging as a crucial transit point for a sophisticated interstate drug trafficking network. Recent seizures reveal traffickers are constantly evolving their methods, using hidden compartments and fake plates to move cannabis from Odisha to northern and western India. Police are targeting the "white-collar drug mafia" operating under legitimate business fronts, with a recent bust uncovering a Rs 4.5 crore racket.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Parents of RG Kar rape-murder victim want CBI to quiz Mamata Banerjee’s nephew
content: Parents of the RG Kar rape-murder victim have accused Dr. Abesh Banerjee, nephew of former CM Mamata Banerjee, of involvement in concealing evidence. They claim his phone was at the hospital on the night of the crime and allege he washed bloodstains in a subsequently demolished bathroom.

title: WC: Messi's Argentina, Mbappe's France in focus as final dash for knockouts begins
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is entering a thrilling group stage finale with 42 nations vying for 29 spots in the Round of 32. Only USA, Mexico, and Germany have secured early qualification, while Haiti, Turkiye, and Tunisia are out. Powerhouses like Argentina, France, England, and Portugal are still fighting for advancement. The week promises intense competition and simultaneous matches as the knockout picture takes shape.

title: Jaipur woman held under UAPA over links to Pakistan-based terror group
content: Rajasthan ATS apprehended a woman in Jaipur for alleged ties with Pakistan-based Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements. Investigations revealed her social media accounts and phone contained objectionable content and links to foreign profiles, including those displaying extremist symbols. She reportedly converted to Islam after guidance from a Pakistan-based Mufti and was allegedly being prepared for travel to Pakistan. Booked under UAPA, she is in ATS custody.

title: DHS to appeal US court on order blocking $100,000 H-1B fee
content: The Department of Homeland Security is urging an appeals court to reinstate a $100,000 H-1B visa fee, arguing a judge's block harms immigration enforcement. The fee, intended to curb foreign worker replacement of Americans, was struck down by a judge citing separation of powers. DHS contends the President has authority for such measures, warning that delays allow more foreign workers entry, potentially causing irreparable damage.

title: 'Fielders didn't support': Defeat to SA leaves Harmanpreet ruing dropped catches
content: "We have two matches and this is the time to stay positive. Shree Charani and Shafali (Verma) bowled well but the fielders didn't support them. We have to take opportunities at this level. We were not lucky enough with that."

title: Man rapes and murders 4-year-old to settle scores with her kin over UP panchayat poll rivalry
content: In a tragic incident in Kasganj district, a four-year-old girl was kidnapped, raped, and murdered, allegedly as an act of revenge linked to a past panchayat election conflict. She was reported missing on June 6, with her body found three days later. Authorities have made one arrest and are pursuing the primary suspect, who allegedly confessed to the brutal crime.

title: At Royal Ascot, India arrives in couture, fascinators and nine yards
content: From royal pageantry and thoroughbred racing to handloom pride and Bollywood glamour, Royal Ascot has increasingly become a stage where Indian identity travels with elegance.

title: Quote of the day by Taylor Swift
content: Taylor Swift, a pop music icon, redefined 'fearless' in her 2008 album 'Fearless'. She believes true bravery isn't the absence of fear, but acting despite it. This philosophy, evident in her lyrics about love, high school, and perseverance, highlights her enduring impact. Swift, now a billionaire, continues to inspire with her candid approach to life's challenges.

title: Starmer steps down as UK PM: From internal revolts to lost confidence, 5 reasons behind the exit
content: Keir Starmer has resigned as Labour leader, citing a need for change after significant election setbacks and internal party dissent. Concerns over his ability to win the next general election, coupled with senior figures openly challenging his leadership and a perceived failure to deliver on 2024 promises, have led to his departure. Scandals also contributed to the pressure for new leadership.

title: West Bengal budget 2026: 20% DA hike, 1 lakh jobs in BJP government's first budget
content: West Bengal's new BJP government has unveiled its inaugural budget, prioritizing welfare and employment. Key announcements include a significant Dearness Allowance hike for state employees, the creation of one lakh government jobs, and a substantial Rs 36,000 crore direct cash transfer scheme for women. The budget also proposes infrastructure development and support for various worker categories, aiming to restore fiscal discipline and public trust.

title: How Delhi's new slum rehabilitation policy aims to reshape housing for families
content: Delhi's new slum policy, approved by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, aims for large-scale redevelopment of informal settlements through public-private partnerships.  The "Jahan Jhuggi Wahi Makan" initiative will provide permanent housing and essential civic infrastructure to an estimated four lakh families, with an extended eligibility cut-off of January 1, 2025. This ambitious urban renewal project seeks to unlock land value for financing and create integrated neighbourhoods.

title: 'Maa Inti Bangaram' BO collection: Samantha Ruth Prabhu film eyes Rs 28–30 crore net
content: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaram' delivered a rare feat over its opening weekend, three consecutive days of growth rather than the usual weekend plateau. After a Rs. 5.35 crore Day 1, the film jumped 43% on Day 2 to Rs. 7.65 crore and a further 32% on Day 3 to Rs. 10.10 crore, taking its three-day domestic net to Rs. 23.10 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 41.79 crore. As the film enters its first Monday, live tracking shows Rs. 0.82 crore collected so far across 1,236 shows, split Rs 0.77 crore in Telugu and Rs. 0.05 crore in Tamil, pushing the cumulative domestic net to Rs. 23.92 crore. With momentum building with each day, B.V. Nandini Reddy's female-led action-family drama looks well positioned for a strong first week.

title: Maharashtra MLC polls results: Full list of winners as Mahayuti wins 16 of 17 seats
content: The ruling Mahayuti alliance has achieved a significant triumph in the Maharashtra Legislative Council elections, securing 16 out of 17 declared seats. The BJP emerged as the dominant force, winning 11 seats, with the Shiv Sena and NCP also contributing to the alliance's success. A notable exception was Nashik, where a BJP rebel secured victory, causing a setback for the Shiv Sena.

title: 'Do people need visa to come to Jantar Mantar?': CJP founder's swipe at Delhi Police
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has accused Delhi Police of obstructing protesters at Jantar Mantar, demanding the Union Education Minister's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak. Dipke alleged police are using Aadhaar card checks to deter participants and highlighted the lack of basic facilities, including water supply to restrooms, for demonstrators. The protest has entered its third day.

title: 'We came with pride, competed with honor': Defiant Iran leave note about 'dignity'
content: After the match at SoFi Stadium, Iran left a handwritten note in their dressing room thanking Los Angeles for hosting them during the tournament. The note was later released by Iran's football federation.

title: How India is managing its oil supply amid Hormuz closure, US-Iran conflict
content: While India continues to rely heavily on energy supplies from the Gulf, the crisis has prompted a faster push towards diversification. Higher crude purchases from Russia, Brazil and Venezuela have helped soften the impact on oil imports.

title: Zakir Khan breaks down as father performs sitar at final Mumbai show
content: In a remarkable farewell performance in Mumbai, comedian Zakir Khan was joined unexpectedly by his father, renowned sitarist Ustad Ismail Khan, on Father's Day. Their emotional duet turned the show into a viral sensation, bringing tears of joy to both Zakir and the audience. As his deeply personal 'Papa Yaar' tour concludes, Zakir prepares for a well-deserved hiatus to focus on his well-being. Read on to know more in detail.

title: ‘This is not IPL’: RR, RCB, DC take a cheeky jibe as Sooryavanshi slams fastest fifty vs SL
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a historic feat, smashing a record 11-ball fifty and a blistering 94 off 29 balls in the Tri-nation final against Sri Lanka A. This remarkable performance, which saw India A clinch the title, served as a powerful response to a previous 'not IPL' jibe.  Sooryavanshi's fearless batting has solidified his reputation as a prodigious talent.

title: 'Op Tiger continues all 365 days': Shinde faction set to induct 6 rebel MPs from Uddhav camp
content: Six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs are officially joining the Eknath Shinde-led faction today, significantly boosting the ruling party's strength in the Lok Sabha. This move marks a major blow to Uddhav Thackeray's party, with the rebels citing ideological differences and proximity to Congress as reasons for their departure. The split intensifies political turmoil, as the Shinde camp welcomes the MPs, continuing its 'Operation Tiger'.

title: Thalapathy Vijay birthday special: 'Not hero material' label to mass Tamil superstar
content: Tamil cinema icon Thalapathy Vijay, once dismissed as "not hero material," has risen to become a bankable star. His journey from early struggles to blockbuster success, marked by iconic roles and the "Thalapathy" title, showcases remarkable resilience. Despite facing controversies and personal challenges, Vijay's enduring popularity has culminated in his becoming the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu in 2026, with his final film, 'Jana Nayagan,' highly anticipated.

title: First round of Iran-US talks in Switzerland: 5 key outcomes include Hormuz, Lebanon
content: Iran and the US have achieved significant progress in high-level talks aimed at ending hostilities and reaching a political settlement. Key outcomes include a 60-day roadmap for a final agreement, the establishment of a Lebanon de-confliction mechanism, and a direct communication line for the Strait of Hormuz. Economic relief for Iran, including eased sanctions and potential asset releases, was also reportedly discussed, with technical negotiations continuing this week.

title: US clears $482 million support package for India's Apaches, M777 howitzers
content: In a significant development, the US has granted formal approval for a $482.2 million package focused on sustainment support services and essential equipment for India's Apache helicopters and M777A2 howitzers. This initiative is crafted to enhance India's defense strength and deepen the strategic alliance between the two nations. The support package is designed to maintain the operational integrity of these vital military assets, catering to India's defense objectives.

title: 'Cocktail 2' BO Day 4 [LIVE]: Will Shahid Kapoor film cross Rs 50 crore today?
content: Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2,' starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, delivered a powerful opening weekend, climbing from Rs 13.50 crore on Day 1 to Rs 47.50 crore net by the end of Day 3, with a worldwide gross crossing Rs 76 crore. As the film enters its first Monday, this would mark the real test for the spiritual sequel to the 2012 classic as the film tries to hold its ground through the weekday grind.

title: Cape Verde script history: First-ever WC goal sets record in Uruguay draw
content: Cape Verde etched their name in World Cup history by scoring their first-ever goal from a direct free-kick against Uruguay, securing a 2-2 draw. This remarkable debutant nation, with two points from two games, continues their fairytale campaign, showcasing resilience and belief against football giants.  The match also featured a rare goalkeeping milestone with both keepers over 40.

title: ITR filing: Switched jobs? How to file tax return and mistakes to avoid
content: One of the most common mistakes employees make is failing to disclose details of salary income earned from their previous employer and the tax already deducted thereon to their new employer.

title: Fellow resident chases, thrashes feeder with stick in Noida’s Gaur City 2 over stray dogs
content: A Gaur City 2 resident was assaulted with a wooden stick and threatened with a scooter while feeding community dogs, sustaining a fractured finger and leg injuries. The accused, Deepak Chauhan, claims self-defense against aggressive dogs, but CCTV footage reportedly shows him attacking the woman. This incident highlights ongoing tensions between dog feeders and some residents in Delhi-NCR, with activists urging authorities for better protection and sensitization.

title: Couple gets Rs 1.05 cr refund after developer sold their flat to someone else
content: A Maharashtra couple secured a major win after a consumer commission ordered a developer to repay Rs 1.05 crore plus interest. The couple paid Rs 90 lakh for a Mumbai flat in 2013 but never received possession. Shockingly, the developer secretly sold the same property to another buyer. Bounced cheques and broken promises led the couple to legal action, culminating in this significant consumer court victory.

title: Watch: India stun China to win 4x100m gold with season-best time at Asian C'ships
content: India's women's 4x100m relay team blazed to gold at the Asian Relay Championships 2026, clocking a season-best 43.85 seconds. Tamanna and Sneha Shanuvalli also contributed to a mixed 4x100m bronze. While sprint relays shone, the 4x400m teams faced challenges. Overall, India secured one gold, one silver, and one bronze, showcasing a mixed but promising performance ahead of major events.

title: Bollywood actor Pankaj Tripathi’s brother attacked with axe outside house in Bihar; 1 arrested
content: Pankaj Tripathi's brother, Bijendra Nath Tiwari, has been critically injured in a sharp-edged weapon attack in Bihar's Gopalganj district. Officials suspect an old dispute led to the assault, which necessitated his transfer to Patna for advanced medical care. Police have registered a case and formed a special team to apprehend the perpetrators, with senior officials overseeing the investigation.

title: 'Operation Keechad': Congress targets BJP after Shinde camp triggers new Uddhav Sena crisis
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera has strongly criticized the BJP, dubbing the recent defection of Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs to Eknath Shinde's faction as "Operation Keechad." He questioned the BJP's motives behind allegedly "stealing" MPs from opposition parties, suggesting it could be an attempt to alter the constitution. This move follows a similar setback for the Trinamool Congress in West Bengal, where several MPs also shifted allegiance.

title: 'I’m a failure as captain': Athapaththu's pain spills out as SL stare at T20 WC exit
content: "Actually, it's a sadness beyond words," Athapaththu, who is playing her tenth T20 World Cup, said after the defeat. "I think I played around 18 years for the national team and I could never get a chance to take my team into a semi-final of a World Cup."

title: Millions of fentanyl pills, no seizures? DEA faces scrutiny over New Mexico operations
content: US drug agents reportedly let thousands of fentanyl pills enter New Mexico communities while pursuing larger drug trafficking rings. Critics argue this tactic endangered lives in a state already battling a severe opioid crisis. The DEA defends its actions, stating investigations were court-authorized and aimed at dismantling major criminal organizations, asserting that targeting kingpins ultimately saves more lives.

title: Morocco script history, surpass Italy and Argentina with record unbeaten streak
content: Morocco have achieved a historic milestone, extending their unbeaten international football run to an incredible 39 matches, setting a new world record. This remarkable streak surpasses legendary runs by Italy and Argentina. The Atlas Lions' success is built on a disciplined defense, tactical prowess, and a belief that has transformed them into genuine contenders for major silverware.

title: 'In US they say Trump follows me, in Israel they say I follow him': Netanyahu on who influences whom
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu asserted his nation's independent decision-making, refuting claims of US President Trump's influence. He stressed that both leaders prioritize their countries' interests, often agreeing but sometimes differing while respecting each other's sovereignty. Netanyahu reiterated Israel's unwavering stance against Iran acquiring nuclear weapons, attributing past successes in preventing this to Israeli military actions and highlighting damage to Tehran's capabilities.

title: Why a 24-year-old founder is taking India's most ambitious data storage bet?
content: The relocation of BioCompute founder Anagha Rajesh to San Francisco has reignited the debate around India's deep-tech ecosystem and brain drain. Despite building a pioneering DNA data storage startup in Bengaluru, Rajesh believes breakthrough innovations require patient capital and long-term vision. Her move highlights a critical challenge for India: retaining ambitious scientific entrepreneurs by creating an environment where bold, high-risk ideas can thrive.

title: Hydroponic weed worth ₹4.83 crore seized at Delhi airport; passenger arrested
content: The passenger arrived from Bangkok on Air India flight AI 2335 on June 21 and was intercepted after crossing the Green Channel based on profiling, an official statement said

title: Akashvani broadcasts ‘yoga anthem’ written by Shivamogga Central Prison inmate
content: The song acknowledges the efforts of Director General of prisons Alok Kumar in promoting yoga and meditation among inmates as part of prisoner welfare and rehabilitation programmes

title: Bengaluru woman conned into opening mule accounts for cyber crime after being cheated of ₹7 lakh with promise of online work
content: The woman woman came in contact with the trio after clicking on an online link about two years ago while browsing social media

title: Kerala HC admits State’s appeal seeking enhancement of punishment in actor assault case
content: The State’s plea was to increase the punishments imposed on the convicted persons, including Sunil N.S., a.k.a. Pulsar Suni and to challenge the acquittal of four of the accused, including actor Dileep

title: Fire breaks out at 3-storey commercial building in Lucknow’s Aliganj; 14 fire tenders deployed
content: No casualty has been reported so far and there is no information about anyone being trapped inside the building, the official said

title: What could Karnataka gain and lose if Tata Power gets a distribution licence?
content: Electricity supply in Karnataka is a system run by Escoms, where each area is assigned a fixed distribution company based on geography, with no option to switch even if the service is unsatisfactory

title: El Niño, a deficient monsoon, and the heat to come
content: With Andhra Pradesh facing a monsoon deficit, meteorologists examine the emerging phenomenon, rising temperatures, and the implications for agriculture, water resources, and public health

title: A ‘historic’ move fails to deliver results for AMMA
content: The series of events that led to the resignation of the film artistes forum’s executive committee reveals that not many wanted the regime headed by its first woman president to last long. Also, the organisation failed to resolve through talks even allegations of a serious nature

title: Telangana CM seeks passenger terminal, MRO and other amenities at proposed airport in Adilabad
content: Mr. Revanth Reddy assures Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh of full cooperation from Telangana Government for the joint use project

title: Three dead, over 15 injured in APSRTC bus-truck collision on NH-716 near Tirupati
content: Among the dead were a nine-month-old child and two teenage girls; the bus was heading from Kadapa to Tirupati when a truck rammed into it

title: Nine detained over suspected impersonation during NEET-UG re-exam in Bihar's Lakhisarai
content: Police were also questioning 10 to 12 other persons allegedly linked to the racket, including biometric operators and middlemen, the Superintendent of Police said

title: Are India’s airports ready to take off?
content: The new terminals at Navi Mumbai, Guwahati and Bengaluru are raking in awards for design. But despite the many innovations at Indian airports, are we on a par with the rest of the world?

title: West Bengal Budget highlights: State Finance Minister pledges to fill 1 lakh government vacancies, 33% for women
content: Finance Minister Swapan Dasgupta announced 20% hike in DA for govt. employees, and a ₹36,000-crore outlay for the financial assistance scheme for women, Annapurna Yojana; Bengal government to revive Calcutta stock exchange

title: TMC’s Aroop Biswas appears before police in Messi event irregularities probe
content: The event organiser Dutta alleged that nearly 22,000 tickets for the event were sold in the black market and claimed that Mr. Biswas used his position as Sports Minister to exert undue influence over the programme

title: What is ammonia poisoning? | Explained
content: Five people died and many were hospitalised after an ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing plant in Tiruvallur

title: Dongria Kondh of Niyamgiri: Where forests, food and faith shape daily life
content: An intimate glimpse into the lives of the Dongria Kondh, one of India’s Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups, in Odisha’s Niyamgiri hills

title: T.N. Governor deserves appreciation for learning Tamil: Speaker
content: Reacting to DMDK leader Premallatha Vijayakant’s allegation that the Governor had mispronounced Tamil words on 37 occasions during his speech, the Speaker pointed out the Governor hailed from another State and should be commended for his efforts to learn the language

title: Entire political science of data doctoring: Congress slams govt on rural wage figures
content: In reality, an analysis of wages data shows that the genuine wage growth would have been around 4.3% per annum, which is the weakest growth in four years, Jairam Ramesh said

title: Gade Sai Krishna case: SIT records mother’s statement, Krishnalanka residents protest for suspended CI
content: The four-member team, formed a day earlier, recorded G. Vijaya Lakshmi’s account at Krishnalanka, where residents blocked the highway backing the suspended Circle Inspector

title: A pictorial journey into 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing'
content: This year’s theme for the International Day of Yoga is “Yoga for Healthy Ageing,” highlighting its role in promoting physical health, mental well-being, emotional resilience and active ageing, contributing to an improved quality of life.

title: VHP alleges 'communal discrimination' at NEET centres, seeks probe
content: VHP national spokesperson Vinod Bansal also sought a detailed explanation from the NTA on the dress code norms followed at exam centres

title: Opposition LDF walks out of Assembly alleging govt failure in controlling disease spread
content: Kerala Health Minister Muraleedharan accused the previous LDF Government of not taking sufficient steps for disease prevention, including in cleaning activities, during the earlier part of the year when it was in charge

title: Sivakumar K.B. takes charge as managing director of KSRTC in Karnataka
content: Prior to his new appointment, Mr. Sivakumar was serving as the Managing Director of Bengaluru Metropolitan Transport Corporation (BMTC)

title: Maharashtra MLC polls: Mahayuti sweeps Legislative Council polls
content: MVA gets zero and the BJP rebel independent candidate wins Nashik; Shiv Sena UBT expels a candidate for anti-party activities after his defeat

title: Industries Secretary submits unconditional apology in Kerala High Court
content: The current Secretary in charge of the Cashew Development Department sought time to reconsider the denial of sanction for a CBI probe into the alleged misappropriation

title: AIADMK legislators walk out of Tamil Nadu Assembly over Mekedatu issue
content: The Speaker said he could not permit the discussion on Mekedatu as the Business Advisory Committee had decided there would be neither Question Hour nor Zero Hour during the sitting; the AIADMK MLAs staged a walkout in protest

title: Odisha Speaker Surama Padhy dismisses BJD, Congress petitions seeking disqualification of 11 MLAs
content: Both parties alleged that the conduct of the MLAs, which was inconsistent with the mandate on which they were elected as BJD candidates, amounted to a voluntary relinquishment of their party membership, thereby leading to disqualification

title: Tiruvallur ammonia leak: Death toll rises to five at seafood processing unit as three more women workers succumb
content: The Health department continues to closely monitor the situation and ensure the availability of appropriate medical care for all affected persons; it is coordinating with the district authorities and healthcare institutions to facilitate timely treatment, surveillance and response activities

title: 1-km ropeway to anchor ₹56.81-crore Bhongir Fort makeover; Jupally displeased over delays in execution
content: ‘Bhongir Fort Experiential Zone’ aims to preserve the heritage character of the fort while creating modern infrastructure and improving accessibility and visitor experience

title: ‘Operation Tiger’ successful, declares Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde
content: “Those who know me, know that we do not leave any operation incomplete,” says Shinde; all six MPs of Shiv Sena UBT are slated to merge with Shiv Sena, say party sources

title: Kerala HC dismisses PIL challenging free travel for women, transgender persons in KSRTC ordinary buses
content: The Court observed that there was nothing in the plea to prove that a Government Order that had been issued with regard to the free travel scheme, was illegal

title: Hundreds of farmers join ‘Bairamangala Chalo’ rally against Bidadi Township Project
content: The rally marks the first large-scale farmers protest against the project, although local protests are ongoing for over 460 days.

title: Ammonia leak: Committee to inspect hazardous industries in Tamil Nadu
content: Making a suo motu statement in the Legislative Assembly on the death of five women workers following the ammonia gas leak tragedy, the Labour Minister said CM Vijay had already issued orders for the formation of the committee

title: Jairam Ramesh writes to Sonowal, seeks clarifications on Great Nicobar project's transhipment port
content: Is private ownership of ports to be diversified, or will the airports-like situation be permitted, where only one private company has acquired the six airports sold off so far, Congress leader Jairam Ramesh asked

title: Telangana SIR: Concerns over voting rights of Gulf migrants
content: The delegation suggested organising online awareness programmes through Zoom meetings and social media platforms to reach Telangana citizens living in Gulf countries and other parts of the world

title: SIR: Karnataka-endorsed residential certificate not valid under Election Commission guidelines
content: With door-to-door visits scheduled to start across Karnataka from June 30 as part of the Special Intensive Revision, the State government has directed Panchayat Development Officers to issue residential certificates to those who require them as supporting documents for the exercise

title: Four killed, seven injured as truck collides with van in Madhya Pradesh's Chhindwara
content: The accident occurred on the Chhindwara-Betul National Highway near Tamri village, about 30 km from the district headquarters, police said

title: Cochin Minerals and Rutile pay-off case: ED questions company CFO
content: The financial manager and a senior clerk of CMRL are also likely to appear before the ED on June 23, 2026

title: Fertiliser to be allotted online this Kharif in Vizianagaram, says Collector
content: The Collector said the online system would bring transparency and ease crowding at outlets; the district needs about 88,000 tonnes of fertiliser this Kharif season

title: AMMA crisis: Let people with conviction and empathy lead the association, says actor Ansiba Hassan
content: She alleged that the executive committee, which stepped down on Sunday (June 21, 2026), failed to present a proper explanation on the gaps detected in the financial affairs of the association.

title: Over 20 lakh medical aspirants appear for NEET-UG 2026 re-exam
content: Around 7 lakh officials, including police teams, observers and examination staff, were mobilised across India to conduct this examination

title: CJP protesters remain at Jantar Mantar for third consecutive day
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke announced that the protest would continue on Monday, June 22, and appealed to farmer unions and members of the public to join the movement.

title: NTA dismisses video claiming paper leak as 'fake', says NEET re-exam successfully held
content: The NTA appealed to students, parents and the public to rely only on the official website and the agency's verified social media handles for information and not to amplify such material

title: Four of a family found dead in Chittoor; police suspect financial distress
content: The police suspect financial strain from prolonged cancer treatment, after a farm worker, his wife and their two children were found dead in Chittoor

title: Bangladeshi among nine wildlife traffickers held in Assam
content: One of the eight golden langurs recovered from their possession was found dead. Officials say the animal is an endangered species with a blackmarket value of about ₹16 lakh, adding that the seized animals will be released in their natural habitat soon

title: Congress MP Dean Kuriakose flays removal of Kerala representative from Mullaperiyar panel
content: Congress MP Dean Kuriakose criticizes the NDSA’s removal of Kerala’s representative from the Mullaperiyar Dam safety panel

title: Chief Minister and former actor Vijay turns 52: a collection of stories on the TVK chief
content: Affectionately called ‘Thalapathy’ by his fans, Vijay was widely considered one of the most successful actors in Tamil cinema before he made his historical plunge into politics

title: Andhra Pradesh Information Commission bars RTI activist for ‘misuse’, orders record seizure
content: The Commission dismissed 39 appeals by advocate Perumal Jayachandra Reddy, citing misuse, vexatious litigation and allegations that he took money from information seekers

title: Decoupling footpaths from accidents
content: Justice Narasimha’s judgment divorces the footpath from the theme of motor accidents. The sole objective of a footpath is not to avoid accidents. The pathways have an identity and a greater purpose of their own

title: Madras High Court takes up suo motu case to protect people from stray dogs in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry
content: The case has been taken up in compliance of a directive of the Supreme Court which had held that compassion for animal life cannot be interpreted in a manner that compels citizens to endure recurring threats to their own lives

title: Smartphones, Laptops to Get More Expensive As AI's Latest Side Effects
content: AI's latest side effect? More expensive smartphones, laptops and gaming consoles like PlayStation. Read on to know what's driving up these gadget prices.

title: How Amitav Ghosh's Book On Colonial Indonesia Inspired A Kerala Budget Policy
content: The Chief Minister specifically cited the proposed International Maritime Museum near Muziris as an initiative influenced by his reading.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps Over 290 Points Amid US-Iran Talks in Switzerland
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices climb 2% as Trump issues threat amid US-Iran peace talks; Brent at $82 per barrel.

title: What UK PM Keir Starmer Said In His Resignation Speech: Top Quotes
